fields.title: Epic: Sandbox failure diagnostics for model clarity
fields.description:
When commands fail inside sandbox, the model often sees cryptic errors like 'Could not resolve host' or 'Permission denied' without understanding these are sandbox restrictions, not real system issues.

Problem:
- ASRT removes network namespace, so network blocks surface as DNS failures
- Model misdiagnoses sandbox denials as 'DNS broken' or 'system issue'
- No structured way for model to know WHY a command failed

Solution:
- Classify sandbox failures by type (network/filesystem) and subtype (dns/blocked/write)
- Gate diagnostics using known sandbox config (reduce false positives)
- Emit clear diagnostic message into tool output stream
- Optionally include machine-readable JSON blob

Ref: ASRT design uses proxies outside sandbox via Unix sockets, so network denial = no network namespace = DNS-like errors.
fields.close_reason: Implemented sandbox failure diagnostics: structured classifier + human + JSON emission, verified ASRT allow-all semantics
